back_translation: Am I not a free person? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus the Lord of us all? Are you all yourselves not the work-product of mine in the Lord?
If for others I am not an apostle, at least for you all I am; because you all are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
This is the defense of mine to those who are scrutinizing me.
Do we not have the right to eat and drink?
Do we not have the right to take along a wife who is also a sister-in-faith — just as the other apostles do, and the (royal) brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?
Or are only myself and Barnabas the ones who do not have the right not to have to work for a living?
Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and does not drink the milk of that flock?
Am I speaking these things only from a human perspective? Or does the Law not also say the same thing?
Because in the law of Moses there is a written word: "Do not put a muzzle on the mouth of an ox while it is treading out the grain." Is God concerned about the oxen?

Or is He speaking that primarily for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake — that the one who plows should plow with hope, and the one who threshes the grain ought to thresh with the hope of receiving a share.
If we have sown things of the spirit among you all, is it a great matter that we will reap things of the flesh from you all?

If others have a share in this right over you, should we not have it even more? But we have not used this right; rather, we endure all things, in order not to cause any obstruction to the good news of Christ.
Do you all not know that those who do the work in matters of the temple eat the food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share together in what is upon the altar?
Likewise, the Lord has (royally) ordained that those who proclaim the good news should also receive their living from the good news.
But I myself have not used any of these rights at all. And I am not writing these things so that they would be done for me — because I would rather die than to have anyone make my boast empty.
Because if I proclaim the good news, there is no boast for me; because there is an obligation pressing upon me — woe to me if I do not proclaim the good news!
Because if I do this thing willingly, I have a reward; but if unwillingly, I have still received an entrusted responsibility.
Then what is my reward? It is that, while proclaiming the good news, I will offer the good news without charging any price — and so not use up my right in the good news to the full.
Because even though I am free from all people, I have made myself a slave of all, in order to win the hearts of as many as I am able to.

I became like a Jew toward the Jews, in order to win the hearts of the Jews. Toward those who are under the Law, I was like one under the Law — although I myself am not under the Law — in order to win the hearts of those who are under the Law.

Toward those who do not have the Law, I was like one who does not have the Law — although I am not without the Law of God, but am under the Law of Christ — in order to win the hearts of those who do not have the Law.
Toward those who are weak, I was a weak person, in order to win the hearts of those who are weak. I have become all things toward all people, in order that at least I might save some people.
I do all these things for the sake of the good news, in order that I myself might have a share in the good news.
Do you all not know that in the contest at the racecourse, all the runners run, but only one person receives the prize? Run in such a manner so as to receive the prize.
Everyone who competes also trains himself in self-control in every matter. Those people do it in order to receive a crown that can decay; but we do it for one that does not decay.
Therefore I run, but not in an aimless way; I box, but not in the manner of striking the air.
Rather, I beat my body and bring it under control like a slave, so that after I have proclaimed to others, I myself will not become one who is rejected as not approved.
